arts.ttl: What Nathan Jones and every League One boss has said about Birmingham City
arts.sum: Here is everything the eight League One managers who have faced Blues this season have said, plus the thoughts of Charlton Athletic manager Nathan Jones whose team are next up... But it’s played by 11 players and about which team is better not how much money you spend.”

arts.ttl: Birmingham beaten as Matty Godden strike gives Charlton victory
arts.sum: The Blues did not register a shot on target.

arts.ttl: Charlton Athletic v Birmingham City team line-ups: Five changes made by Nathan Jones for Valley clash
arts.sum: Charlton: Mannion, Ramsay, Mitchell, Jones, Docherty, Coventry, Docherty, A Campbell, Anderson, Kanu, Godden. Birmingham start with two former Charlton players in their ranks – Alfie May and Krystian Bielik.

arts.ttl: Hansson returns: Birmingham City team news and predicted XI to face Charlton Athletic
arts.sum: The latest Birmingham City team news as Chris Davies' side gear up to face Charlton Athletic in League One. The post Hansson returns: Birmingham City team news and predicted XI to face Charlton Athletic appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Saturday's League One predictions including Charlton Athletic vs. Birmingham City
arts.sum: Sports Mole provides score predictions and previews for all of today's League One fixtures, including Charlton Athletic vs. Birmingham City.

arts.ttl: Garry Cook reveals Birmingham City have ‘doubled’ target as Knighthead accelerate plans
arts.sum: Blues fans flocked to buy season tickets during the close season, with in excess of 18,000 sold, and they have been rewarded by a rampant start from Chris Davies’ new-look team. Blues are, of course, destined to leave St Andrew’s in the future after Knighthead purchased 60 acres of land in east Birmingham to build a new sports quarter.

arts.ttl: Chris Davies opens up on Ayumu Yokoyama snubs and sets Birmingham City winger a challenge
arts.sum: Davies has already taken issue with the 18-man squad rule in League One and that hasn’t worked in Yokoyama’s favour. Opening up on why Yokoyama has been overlooked of late, Davies told BirminghamLive: “From his point of view, he’s still adapting.

arts.ttl: Chris Davies gives Ethan Laird return timeline in full Birmingham City injury update
arts.sum: Davies hopes Laird, who hasn’t played since late-August due to an ankle injury, will have returned to full training by the end of the upcoming international break. “I’m hoping Laird will be much more active in terms of potentially getting involved in training by the end of the international break,” said Davies.

arts.ttl: I hope Birmingham City boss Chris Davies doesn’t get the Pep Guardiola treatment
arts.sum: “The way Birmingham City play all comes from Chris Davies,” ex-Wolves, West Bromwich Albion and Walsall striker Goodman told Grosvenor Sport. While Davies has banked credit from those inside the Blues bubble, outsiders will point to the money the club spent during the summer transfer window.

arts.ttl: Charlton Athletic defender respectful of Birmingham frontline but fired up by challenge
arts.sum: The Midlands club, relegated from the Championship, are five points clear of second-placed Wrexham and have scored the most goals in England’s third tier – 17 in eight games. “There has been a lot of talk about them at the moment, I’d love to be the team that maybe quietens that down a little bit.
